content.problemStatement:
**"Ron : Harry!!! Let's go!!"**


Ron and Harry just entered Aragog's Colony. Some hungry children of Aragog come running to them for food. Suddenly they see a bright light emerging from nothingness. From the bright light a tiny, cute and charming angel named **Sam** emerged. She promised to rescue them, if they make her a wand of exactly length **N**.


**Sam** had **K** different types of wands having different lengths. Each type of wand having infinite supply. Harry can join/combine any two wands end to end to make a newer wand, i.e. if two wands of length x and y are joined, then the length of the new wand will be x + y. Now Harry is interested in knowing the minimum and the maximum number of wands he can join in order to produce a wand of length **N**. If it is not possible to produce such wand then print **-1** in both cases.
constraints: All values of lengthi are distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line of input contains two space separated integers N and K denoting the desired wand length and number of types of wands available. The second line contains K space separated integers where ith integer is lengthi, the length of ith type of wand.
content.outputFormat: Print two space separated integers in one line where first and second integer denotes the minimum and maximum number of wands needed to join to form a wand of length N respectively. Print -1 in both cases if wand of length N cannot be formed.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Join wands of length 2 and 5 to produce a wand of length 7. Join wands of length 2, 2 and 3 to produce a wand of length 7.
It is not possible to produce a wand of length 4 using the given wand lengths.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to find the minimum and maximum number of wands needed to form a wand of length N. We will create two arrays, minWands and maxWands, where minWands[i] and maxWands[i] will store the minimum and maximum number of wands needed to form a wand of length i respectively. We will iterate over all possible lengths from 1 to N and for each length, we will check all possible wand lengths. If the current length is equal to the wand length, we will update minWands and maxWands accordingly. If the current length is greater than the wand length, we will check if we can form a wand of the remaining length and update minWands and maxWands accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N*K) — where N is the desired wand length and K is the number of types of wands available. This is because we are iterating over all possible lengths from 1 to N and for each length, we are checking all possible wand lengths.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the desired wand length. This is because we are using two arrays, minWands and maxWands, of size N to store the minimum and maximum number of wands needed to form a wand of each length.
